title: campanula rapunculus subsp. verruculosa
intro: Campanula rapunculus subsp. verruculosa, also known as the Harebell or Bluebell of Scotland, is a plant species belonging to the bellflower family. The roots of Campanula rapunculus subsp. verruculosa contain saponins, which are known for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, the roots of Campanula rapunculus subsp. verruculosa have been used in herbal preparations like infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
